_meta.user_context: 27yo single male ER physician, starting at Credit Valley Hospital July 3 2026, recently out of LTR, west-GTA apartment search
_meta.data_vintage: Statistics Canada 2021 Census + neighbourhood profiles + AreaVibes + city-of-Toronto/Mississauga open data; supplemented by 2026 condo-density estimates
_meta.caveats: Census data is 5 years old (2021). Newer condo towers (Humber Bay, Mimico, Square One District) shift demographics younger. Estimated fields flagged with _estimated:true.

Central Erin Mills / Sawmill Valley.honest_vibe: Affluent South-Asian/Chinese immigrant family suburbia anchored on Credit Valley Hospital and Erin Mills Town Centre. Tons of medicine, very few singles. You'll see colleagues but not dates.

Streetsville.honest_vibe: Charming heritage main-street village with a few pubs (Franklin House, Crooked Cue). Median age 8% above Mississauga. Families and older couples, very small singles pool.

Cooksville / Square One.predominant_ethnicity: South Asian 25%, Arab 12%, Chinese 10%, Filipino 8%, White ~30%
Cooksville / Square One.honest_vibe: The closest thing Mississauga has to an urban core. Square One condo towers are packed with 25-35yo immigrant-background professionals, students, and renters. Real bar/restaurant density (Celebration Square, Square One District). Cooksville proper skews older; the tower cluster is the dating zone.

Port Credit.honest_vibe: Lake-village vibe with a real bar strip (Door FiftyFive, Ten, Canvas, Nomads, Pump House). Skews 30-45 mixed couples + divorced + young families, but enough singles patio scene to actually meet people. Best Mississauga option for a 27yo not in a tower.

Clarkson / Lorne Park.honest_vibe: Old-money lakefront detached-home country. Median age 11% above Mississauga, lowest density in the city. This is dentists-with-kids territory. Dating dead zone.

Applewood.honest_vibe: Quiet 1960s post-war family suburb sandwiched between Cooksville and the Etobicoke border. Strip-mall amenities, no walkable nightlife. Convenient to CVH (~15 min) but you'll drive everywhere and meet nobody.

Markland Wood / Centennial Park.honest_vibe: 43% families-with-kids, 25% couples-no-kids, only 27% one-person-households. Golf course, schools, ravine. Classic established detached-home suburbia. Dead for dating, peaceful for sleep.

West Mall / Eringate.honest_vibe: Affordable first-time-buyer/young-family pocket between the 427 and Etobicoke Creek. Some rental apartments but vibe is utilitarian suburban. No reason for a single 27yo to live here unless cheap rent.

Stonegate-Queensway.honest_vibe: The Queensway strip has matured into a legitimate restaurant row (Cibo, Tutto Bene, San Remo, the IKEA-adjacent breweries). Median age only 2% above Toronto. Mix of young couples buying their first house and condo dwellers. Not Liberty Village, but solidly walkable.

Mimico.predominant_ethnicity: White ~65%, East Asian 10%, South Asian 8%, E.European 6%
Mimico.honest_vibe: Lakeshore strip + Royal York/Mimico GO corridor. 35% of residents are 25-39 per realtor profiles. Heavy condo dweller mix of young pros + young couples. Birds and Beats, Eden Trattoria, Sanremo Bakery, plus Humber Bay nightlife two stops east. Real singles density.

Long Branch / New Toronto.honest_vibe: Up-and-coming streetcar-era waterfront pocket. Mix of bungalow families + young pros moving west for cheaper Lakeshore condos. Smaller scene than Mimico/Humber Bay but a real one. Skews slightly more 'settling-down couples' than 'single 27yo'.

Humber Bay Shores.predominant_ethnicity: White ~55%, East Asian 18%, South Asian 8%, E.European 6%
Humber Bay Shores.honest_vibe: The west-GTA dating capital. Dense condo cluster of 25-40yo finance/tech/healthcare renters. Eden Trattoria, IQ Food, Birds and Beats, lakefront patios. ~46% one-person households per profiles. Highest Hinge/Bumble density west of King West. THE answer for a 27yo single ER doc with a Mercedes-to-CVH commute.

Kipling / Islington.honest_vibe: Transit-anchored mid-density node at the western end of the Bloor subway. New condo towers (IQ, On The Park) bringing in young renters, but vibe is still 60% mature Italian/Polish family + 40% young condo dweller. Decent transit-to-King-West means you can date downtown easily.

Old Mill / Kingsway / Bloor West edge.honest_vibe: Wealthy Anglo-establishment professional families. Bloor West Village is charming (Magwyer's, Tati Bistro, Eclectic Cafe) but the demographic is married 35-55 dual-income parents. Pretty for walks, terrible for meeting singles.

High Park / Roncesvalles / Junction.predominant_ethnicity: White ~75%, East Asian 8%, South Asian 5%, Latin American 4%
High Park / Roncesvalles / Junction.honest_vibe: Roncesvalles + Junction is one of the best-loved young-professional-with-or-without-baby corridors in Toronto. Heavy 30-something density, real cafe/bar/restaurant scene (Bandit Brewery, Indie Alehouse, Hole In The Wall). 59% married couples means lots of couples but ALSO their single friends. Commute to CVH is 30-40 min though.

Liberty Village / King West.predominant_ethnicity: White ~60%, East Asian 15%, South Asian 10%, Black 5%
Liberty Village / King West.honest_vibe: Reference point. Median age 18% below Toronto, density 195% above. Tech/finance/marketing 25-35yo singles paradise. The downside is your CVH commute becomes 40-55 min each way. Trade-off: maximum dating market vs maximum drive time.

ranked_for_user.why: Maximum singles density west of Yonge but worst commute (~45 min to CVH). Reference ceiling.
Best balance: dense young-pro tower cluster, real nightlife, 25-30 min drive to CVH, 18 min transit to King West. The obvious answer.
Authentic 30-something neighborhood, character + density + restaurants. Commute to CVH ~35 min QEW.
Slightly less dense than Humber Bay but cheaper and adjacent. Same lakefront condo demo, shorter CVH commute (~22 min).
Closest urban-core feel inside Mississauga, 15-min CVH commute. Demographic skews immigrant-family but condo towers concentrate young renters. Real bar/restaurant density.
Best lake-village vibe, real bar strip, 15-20 min to CVH. Skews 30-45 but lively patio scene.
Quietly maturing Queensway restaurant row, easy transit to King West, but slightly family-skewed.
Transit-good but vibe still half mature-family suburb. Backup option.
Up-and-coming but smaller, more 'settling-down couples' than singles.
Charming village, demographically dead for a 27yo.
Adjacent to CVH and convenient but it's South-Asian family suburbia. You'd be living next to your hospital and almost no peers.
Bland 1960s family suburb, no walkable scene.
Old-money lakefront detached-home country. Median age 45+. Dead.

top_5_dating: Liberty Village / King West (reference, commute penalty)
honest_caveats: Census data is from 2021 (5 years old). Newer condo towers in Humber Bay Shores, Mimico, and Square One District have shifted those demographics meaningfully younger and more single since then.

Mississauga overall median age is 40.3 with most neighborhoods skewing toward married South-Asian/Chinese/Arab families with kids. The two exceptions are Square One condo cluster (young renter towers) and Port Credit (mixed, with real nightlife).

Many fields are estimates extrapolated from federal-electoral-district profiles and AreaVibes neighborhood pages. Exact dissemination-area numbers would shift specific percentages by ~5pp but rankings would not change.
The single largest variable for a 27yo ER doc is COMMUTE vs DATING POOL. Humber Bay Shores is the Pareto-optimal point: 25-30 min to CVH off-peak, dense singles scene, ferry-quick to King West.
Etiquette warning: if you live in Central Erin Mills or Streetsville you will run into co-residents, attendings, and patients constantly. May matter post-breakup.

predominant_ethnicity is included because dating-market composition meaningfully varies (Erin Mills is South-Asian dominant, Roncesvalles is Anglo-European, Square One is multiethnic). No value judgment, just signal.
